title: Cultural Trade Promotion Act
plain_summary: This bill helps small creative businesses like artists, musicians, and craft makers sell their products to other countries. It requires government trade agencies to give special help to these small businesses so they can export their goods more easily. The bill also wants better shipping services for small businesses and adds someone who represents creative industries to an important tourism board. The goal is to help American creative businesses grow by selling their arts, crafts, and cultural products around the world.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Cultural Trade Promotion Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Definitions
sections.text: In this law, creative industry or occupation means two things. First, it means an industry that has a big current or future impact on a state, regional, or local economy or a Native American community's economy. This includes jobs that help people earn enough money to support themselves and get promoted. These industries help grow businesses, nonprofit groups, or self-employment opportunities that come from individual creativity, skill, and talent. This includes businesses and groups focused on design, crafts, music, visual and media arts, performing arts, language, literature, or expressions of Native cultures or regional or local heritage culture. Second, it means an occupation that currently has or is expected to have enough jobs in an industry to make a big potential impact on a state, regional, or local economy or a Native American community's economy. This includes jobs that help people earn enough money to support themselves and get promoted. These occupations are made up of the businesses or nonprofit organizations described above, or individuals who work for themselves and whose work comes from individual creativity, skill, and talent. This includes focus on design, crafts, music, visual arts, media arts, performing arts, language, literature, or expressions of Native cultures or regional or local heritage culture. Native American, when talking about culture, means the culture of a Native American as defined in section one hundred three of the Native American Languages Act.

sections.title: Section 3  Promotion of Exports from Microenterprises and Creative Industries and Occupations
sections.text: This section changes existing trade promotion laws to include very small businesses and creative industries. The United States and Foreign Commercial Service must now help microentrepreneurs (very small business owners as defined in existing law), small businesses, and medium-sized businesses with exports, instead of just helping small and medium-sized businesses. The Trade Promotion Coordinating Committee must create a plan that includes helping microenterprises and considering how to promote exports of goods and services from creative industries and occupations. The law also expands help for Native Hawaiian arts and crafts exports and Native Hawaiian owned businesses. It changes the requirement that items be hand made or hand crafted to just requiring they be made.

sections.title: Section 4  Collaboration to Improve Access to Reliable International Shipping Services
sections.text: The Under Secretary of Commerce for International Trade, the Assistant Secretary of Commerce and Director General of the United States and Foreign Commercial Service, and the Postmaster General must work together. They must figure out how to better connect microenterprises (very small businesses) and small businesses to fast and reliable international shipping services that meet what modern consumers expect.

sections.title: Section 5  Focus on Creative Industries and Occupations by Trade and Development Agency
sections.text: This section changes the Foreign Assistance Act to require the Trade and Development Agency to focus on creative industries and occupations along with environment issues when carrying out its work.

sections.title: Section 6  Travel and Tourism Advisory Board
sections.text: Even if other laws or rules say something different, the Secretary of Commerce must appoint a representative of creative industries and occupations to serve as a permanent member of the United States Travel and Tourism Advisory Board. This board was created under existing laws and helps advise the government on travel and tourism matters.
